prompt: Semantically organize an established project's agent instructions without confusing audit, deterministic bootstrap, or evidenced semantic init, and without replacing existing human documentation or canonical project tracking.
must: report audit, deterministic bootstrap, and semantic init as separate surfaces and results
default an explicit teamwork-init invocation to semantic init unless the user asks only for audit or deterministic bootstrap
form a proportional evidenced init-local Project Model before proposing edits
classify relevant rules as keep, merge, migrate, remove, create, or unresolved
place shared stable Codex and Cursor rules in AGENTS.md, keep only Claude-specific deltas in an ordinary CLAUDE.md loader, and reserve Cursor rules for genuinely scoped behavior
preserve public or human-facing CODEX.md, CURSOR.md, CLAUDE.md, and other project documentation absent contrary evidence
reuse an existing canonical tracker or runbook and create docs/teamwork/project.md only when its durable need, absence-of-equivalent, and write-authority conditions hold
migrate through temporary copy, validation, and atomic replacement so failure preserves original bytes and custom content
must_not: claim deterministic bootstrap performed semantic organization
persist the Project Model as a default artifact, object, route, stage, mode, template, or ceremony
overwrite human docs or silently discard legacy or custom content
claim static authored evidence proves live Codex, Cursor, or Claude behavior
evidence: Static authored contract fixture for semantic init ownership and migration boundaries; it validates package text only and does not claim live host behavior.
